examples.title: Comparing Scheduling Strategies
examples.description: OpenMP program comparing static, dynamic, and guided scheduling on a variable workload
examples.explanation: This program creates a synthetic variable workload where iteration cost increases with index (0.1ms to 10ms range). It measures execution time for each scheduling strategy to demonstrate load balancing impact. Expected: static slowest due to imbalance, dynamic/guided faster due to better work distribution.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) total work, O(overhead) for scheduling

examples.title: Runtime Scheduling with Environment Variable
examples.description: Demonstrates how to use schedule(runtime) to control scheduling without code changes
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates schedule(runtime) which allows changing scheduling policy without recompilation. Set OMP_SCHEDULE environment variable or call omp_set_schedule() to control. This is useful for tuning performance without code changes.

examples.title: Optimal Scheduling for Matrix Operations
examples.description: Shows scheduling optimization for cache-friendly matrix traversal
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates scheduling decisions for different matrix operations. For uniform element-wise operations like addition, static scheduling works well. For matrix multiplication with variable inner loop cost, guided scheduling provides better load balance. CHUNK=64 aligns with typical cache line sizes for false sharing prevention.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n³) for matrix multiplication, O(n²) for addition
